pots_observed.registry_match: P2: Mexican Mint (Black Pot | Soil | Sensor)
Unmonitored: Money Plant (White Cup | Water Propagation | No Sensors)

new_introductions_interventions.item: Unmonitored: Money Plant (White Cup | Water Propagation | No Sensors)
new_introductions_interventions.description: A white cup containing a plant cutting in water was introduced into the biome.
A purple PCB, likely part of the sensor system, became clearly visible near the black pot.

P2_Mexican_Mint.status: Present and accounted for, matching registry.
P2_Mexican_Mint.details: The Mexican Mint in the black pot with soil and sensor is consistently present throughout the sequence.

Unmonitored_Money_Plant.status: Introduced at T-3, matching registry from that point.
Unmonitored_Money_Plant.details: The Money Plant cutting in the white cup for water propagation was not present in the EARLIEST image but appeared at T-3 and remained through CURRENT.

earliest.description: Young plant with 4-5 rounded, turgid, healthy medium-green leaves. Upright posture. Soil appears moist with white fragments (eggshells). Sensor visible.

t_minus_3.description: Plant largely unchanged in size and leaf count (4-5 leaves), maintaining turgidity and green color. Soil surface appears slightly drier, with more visible clumping. White fragments persist. A purple PCB sensor component is now clearly visible.

t_minus_2.description: Leaves still green and turgid, no visible loss or significant growth. Posture consistent. Soil surface texture similar to T-3, possibly marginally drier. Sensor components in place.
t_minus_2.health_inference: Healthy, but soil moisture continues to decrease.

t_minus_1.description: Leaves present, but overall image much darker, making color assessment difficult. Appears to retain shape and turgidity. Soil surface noticeably dry and showing signs of cracking. White fragments still visible.
t_minus_1.health_inference: Stable, but under increasing stress due to very dry soil. Immediate intervention (watering) recommended.

current.description: Extremely dark image. Plant barely discernible as a shadowy outline. No details about color, turgidity, or specific leaf count can be accurately determined. Soil surface completely obscured.
current.health_inference: Indeterminate due to severe lighting limitations. No visible collapse, but no positive signs of health either.

t_minus_3.description: A cutting with a single prominent, healthy-looking green, turgid leaf submerged in clear water within a white plastic cup. Water level high. No roots clearly visible.

t_minus_2.description: Cutting still present with its single prominent green, turgid leaf. Water level appears consistent. No visible changes.

t_minus_1.description: Cutting and leaf still present. Due to darker lighting, green color appears less vibrant, but leaf shape and turgidity seem maintained. Water level appears consistent.

current.description: Cutting barely visible as a dark silhouette in the extremely dark image. No details about health, color, or turgidity can be assessed.
current.health_inference: Indeterminate due to severe lighting limitations.

biome_observations.incidental_growth: No weeds, moss, secondary seedlings, or uncatalogued sprouts observed in either the black pot or white cup throughout the sequence.

biome_anomalies.t_minus_2: Similar to T-3, possibly marginally drier.
biome_anomalies.t_minus_1: Noticeably dry, showing signs of cracking.

biome_anomalies.description: Small white fragments (consistent with eggshells) are present on the soil surface of the black pot from EARLIEST through T-1. Obscured in CURRENT.
Water in the white cup appears clear and free of algae or debris from T-3 through T-1. Obscured in CURRENT.
Desk surface generally clear, apart from the plants, sensor setup, a blue book, and a pen visible in EARLIEST.

earliest_to_t_minus_3.P2_Mexican_Mint: Soil appears slightly drier. A purple PCB component of the sensor became clearly visible.
earliest_to_t_minus_3.Unmonitored_Money_Plant: New introduction of the Money Plant cutting in a white cup with water.

t_minus_3_to_t_minus_2.P2_Mexican_Mint: Minimal change; soil moisture might have decreased slightly.

t_minus_2_to_t_minus_1.P2_Mexican_Mint: Significant darkening of the image. Soil surface appears noticeably drier and possibly cracking.

t_minus_1_to_current.P2_Mexican_Mint: Extreme darkening of the image, rendering most visual details unobservable.
t_minus_1_to_current.Unmonitored_Money_Plant: Extreme darkening of the image, rendering most visual details unobservable.

visual_health_inference.P2_Mexican_Mint: Initially healthy (EARLIEST to T-2), but progressively experienced drying soil conditions (T-1). Current health is indeterminate due to severe visual obstruction.
visual_health_inference.Unmonitored_Money_Plant: Appeared healthy and stable in water propagation from its introduction (T-3) through T-1. Current health is indeterminate due to severe visual obstruction.

anomalies: Introduction of the Money Plant cutting in a white cup at T-3.
Increased visibility of a purple PCB sensor component at T-3.
Progressive drying and cracking of soil in the black pot from T-3 to T-1.
Severe and progressive darkening of images from T-1 to CURRENT, significantly hindering visual assessment.
vision_report.narrative_description: The observation period tracked two plant specimens: a Mexican Mint in a black pot with a sensor, and a Money Plant cutting in a white cup. The Mexican Mint (P2) maintained its leaf count and turgidity, appearing healthy from EARLIEST to T-2. However, its soil progressively dried out, becoming noticeably parched and cracked by T-1, indicating a need for watering. The Money Plant cutting (Unmonitored) was introduced at T-3 and appeared healthy, propagating in water through T-1. A sensor setup is consistently present with the Mexican Mint, and white eggshell fragments are noted on the soil surface of the black pot. No incidental growth or other significant biome anomalies were detected, apart from the changing soil moisture and the introduction of the Money Plant. The primary challenge for this audit is the severe degradation of image quality due to extreme darkening in the later stages (T-1 and CURRENT), making definitive health inferences for the most recent states impossible. While no systemic losses or major structural collapses were observed, the health of both plants in the 'CURRENT' state cannot be confirmed visually.
vision_report.confidence: Medium-High. Initial images provide good detail for assessment. Later images (T-1, CURRENT) are severely underexposed, limiting accurate assessment of color, turgidity, and fine details, thus reducing confidence for the most recent states.
